title: “People in power are failing them”
summary: QAnon has been, at least partially, vindicated by the Epstein files. What does that mean for the broader MAGA movement? Jessica Tarlov and The Daily Show’s Jordan Klepper discuss, on Raging Moderates.
transcript_html:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/pgxxdv6FPPY

Transcript

I am probably gonna regret saying this, but was QAnon right? It seems like they have been pretty vindicated on the Epstein files and it's just had this knock-on effect that they now see some of them Trump as actually part of the Epstein class, right? Cuz he is the one who is blocking the release of these files. >> And and they also thought Donald Trump was the savior who was going to come forward and reveal himself. >> Ain't it cute? >> And that's the thing with Qidon. They they threw a thousand things at the wall. You know, 990 some odd continue to

be disproven or pushed to it. It'll happen next week. It'll happen next week. >> And you're very much the MAGA whisperer of the Daily Show. >> A lot of the people that I talk to are in the MAGA movement because it is their identity and that is more important to them than what politics means to them. In fact, like it's almost insincere to debate politics with so many people who are in that MAGA movement because the politics is just almost the almost the gibberish used to to portray and perform their allegiance to an identity to being a part of something. And a lot of the people I talk to outside of the more

extreme MAGA folks, they just want change. I think that is so American. I think seeing incumbents win is a thing of the past. And yes, we will get into the weeds of what is this party going to do or what have you. I think more often than not, Americans feel like they don't like the status quo. They don't get what they want. They deserve more that they are not getting. And the people in power are failing them and letting them down.
